direction.title: Adaptation of model-architecture-dependent mechanisms to downscaled models
direction.rationale: Within Tree T_0, multiple branches assume that active learning, ensemble, and scaling law mechanisms can be directly inherited by downscaled models without adjustment. This fails to account for structural changes in mechanism effectiveness with model size.
direction.meta_gap: The property that active learning, ensemble, and scaling laws can be applied directly to downscaled models without redesign has not been established.
direction.shared_mechanism: Assuming linear scalability of these mechanisms with model size; ignoring that algorithmic design choices may be size-dependent.
direction.non_obvious_property: Property: monotonic relationship between model size and effectiveness of a mechanism (e.g., active learning query strategies). Obvious composition that fails: use the same active learning algorithm (ActiveLLM) on a small model, which fails because the algorithm relies on a large LLM as oracle. Prior work (e.g., ActiveLLM) does not address how query strategies degrade with smaller models. No formal invariant linking model capacity to instance selection quality exists.

per_trend_predictions.direction: Principled adaptation of active learning and ensemble mechanisms to downscaled audio language models via representation intrinsic dimension, establishing a model-size-invariant scaling law for mechanism effectiveness.
per_trend_predictions.rationale: Recent work highlights the need to move beyond scaling for large language models, but mechanisms for improving smaller models are not well-understood. Intrinsic dimension of hidden representations provides a size-agnostic measure that can guide mechanism design, and audio language models benefit from this due to their unique representational structure.
per_trend_predictions.builds_on: Enough of Scaling LLMs! Lets Focus on Downscaling
per_trend_predictions.persistent_gap: The assumption that active learning, ensemble, and scaling laws transfer linearly to downscaled models without architectural modifications remains unvalidated.
per_trend_predictions.why_still_open: Prior work has not examined the relationship between model size and the effectiveness of these mechanisms; they are typically studied in isolation for large models.
per_trend_predictions.bridge_keep: The goal of improving downscaled models through active learning and ensemble.
per_trend_predictions.bridge_remove: The assumption of linear scalability of mechanism effectiveness with model parameters.
per_trend_predictions.bridge_add: A mechanism that computes the intrinsic dimension of the model's hidden representations and uses it to parameterize the threshold for active learning instance selection and the diversity criterion for ensemble creation, ensuring that the mechanism's budget and hyperparameters scale with representational capacity rather than parameter count.
per_trend_predictions.non_obvious_property: Property: Intrinsic dimension of hidden representations provides a model-size-invariant measure that predicts the marginal benefit of active learning and ensemble methods. Why prior work cannot exploit it: Prior work on active learning for LLMs (e.g., ActiveLLM) and ensemble methods (e.g., Bridging the Gap) rely on heuristics tied to large model behavior (e.g., confidence thresholds) and do not attempt to identify a fundamental measure that generalizes across sizes. Why composition fails: A reviewer might propose 'dimensionality estimation + active learning' — but this composition lacks the theoretical validation that intrinsic dimension is predictive across model sizes; the load-bearing piece is establishing this predictive relationship, which is absent from prior work.
per_trend_predictions.obvious_composition_check: Shortest description as existing methods: 'Intrinsic dimension estimation plus active learning with threshold scaling'. This description omits the crucial step of validating that intrinsic dimension is predictive across model sizes and the theoretical derivation of the scaling law. A domain expert would recognize that prior work on intrinsic dimension (e.g., Li et al., 2018) focuses on understanding model complexity, not on guiding mechanism design. Therefore, the composition is not accurate; the load-bearing piece is the new predictive relationship.
per_trend_predictions.forest_meta_gap: The property that active learning, ensemble, and scaling laws can be applied directly to downscaled models without redesign has not been established.
per_trend_predictions.forest_shared_mechanism: Assuming linear scalability of these mechanisms with model size; ignoring that algorithmic design choices may be size-dependent.
